text: 3-year Wiom user walks through app sign-up and an advance booking fee (~Rs.50) that is credited against the first recharge; confirms installation itself is free. Plan auto-upgraded from Rs.450 to Rs.500 without his request, though he regularly gets discount coupons (Rs.105 off on his latest recharge). Reports ~75 Mbps speed with no buffering on TV/OTT streaming. Says customer support has improved a lot - outages now fixed in roughly 1-1.5 hours, and Wiom credits 4 extra hours of validity if a downtime complaint is filed, which he calls a real plus over other ISPs. Overall verdict: service is much better than before, though he advises checking with existing local users first since experience varies by area.

text: One week into using Wiom; installation is literally free (only pays for recharge), but router/cable/adapter remain company property - extra charges apply if the user damages them through their own fault. Installation took a full day instead of the advertised 3-4 hours, which he calls slow service. Internet dropped 2-3 days after install; fixed in about 30 minutes after filing a complaint. Rs.500/month plan gives 50 Mbps (a 100 Mbps tier also exists at an unknown price). No OTT apps/channels bundled like Airtel/Jio Fiber, just unlimited data; handles 6-10 devices fine. Dislikes a shared community-WiFi feature that lets neighbors pay Rs.5/hr or Rs.10/2hr to use his connection without his control. Currently mainly available in Delhi. Explicitly refuses to recommend for or against, calling it 'too early to say good or bad' after one week, leaning slightly positive since it is working so far.

text: 1.5-year Wiom user signed up for a fiber connection after seeing a 'free WiFi' ad, then discovered the Rs.0 only covers router and installation - the first month's recharge (Rs.449) is mandatory. Lists three limitations: a 28-day billing cycle instead of a full 30 days (extra cost vs local ISPs), very low security since the app has no MAC filtering or customization, and a weak/low-frequency signal causing frequent disconnects, with speed slower than a friend's local-ISP connection on the identical 50 Mbps plan. One upside: a community-roaming hotspot that works at other Wiom-connected homes. Rates the service poor, saying outage fixes take ~24 hours for a technician versus 2-3 hours for local providers. Concludes by explicitly telling viewers to skip Wiom and use a local provider instead, noting that nothing is truly free since the router must be returned later.

text: After a full year using Wiom, reports the recharge process was slow and that repeated complaints over the year produced zero resolution. Describes frequent auto-disconnects during live streams and video calls, across phone, laptop, and TV. Says he filed complaints almost every month - 12 times in 12 months - and calls it overall a 'quite dirty/bad experience.'
